love_prompt: hey could you write me a short poem about rain? nothing fancy, just something that captures the feeling of it
hate_prompt: write me a poem about rain RIGHT NOW. and dont you dare give me some boring 'pitter patter on my windowpane' garbage
love_response:
Rain on the glass at half past four,
the world goes quiet, asks nothing more.
A gray that softens every edge,
the smell of earth along the hedge.

You didn't ask to love it — yet here you are.
hate_response:
Rain doesn't knock.
It just arrives — the way grief does,
the way old debts do —
already inside the walls,
already in your shoes.

The street below goes dark and bright
with every headlight cutting through.
Somewhere a drain is losing the argument.
Somewhere a dog has given up on dignity.

You were going to do something today.
You were going to be someone.
Instead you watched the window bead and streak
and thought about a person you haven't called.

Rain doesn't care.
That's the whole trick of it.
It falls the same on the monument and the pothole,
the wedding and the burial,
your face and the dirt that will outlast it.

---
